subject: Chemistry
topics: p-Block Elements: Group 17 Halogens - preparation, properties and uses of chlorine and hydrochloric acid
Interhalogen compounds: types, preparation and properties of XF, XF3, XF5 and XF7
Oxoacids of halogens: structure, oxidising power and stability comparison
d and f Block Elements: electronic configuration and oxidation states of transition metals
Coordination Compounds: Werner's theory, ligands, coordination number and IUPAC nomenclature
Crystal Field Theory: splitting of d-orbitals in octahedral and tetrahedral fields
Stability of coordination complexes and chelate effect
Biomolecules: structure and function of DNA and RNA, Watson-Crick double helix model
Enzyme kinetics: Michaelis-Menten equation and enzyme inhibition
Vitamins: classification as fat-soluble and water-soluble, deficiency diseases
Nucleic Acids: purine and pyrimidine bases, nucleotides and polynucleotide chain
Polymers: addition and condensation polymerisation mechanisms
Biodegradable and non-biodegradable polymers with examples
Chemistry in Everyday Life: drugs - classification, drug-receptor interaction
Antacids, antihistamines, tranquilizers, analgesics with examples
Cleansing action of soaps and detergents: micelle formation mechanism
Electrochemistry: Kohlrausch's law and its applications
Electrolytic conductance: equivalent conductance, molar conductance
Nernst equation and its applications to cell potentials
Corrosion as an electrochemical process: galvanic corrosion prevention
Chemical Kinetics: integrated rate equations for zero, first and second order reactions
Arrhenius equation: activation energy and temperature dependence
Collision theory of reaction rates
Surface Chemistry: adsorption isotherms - Freundlich and Langmuir
Types of colloids: lyophilic and lyophobic sols, properties and preparation
Tyndall effect, Brownian movement, electrophoresis and coagulation
Emulsions and their types, emulsification
Haloalkanes and Haloarenes: SN1 and SN2 mechanisms with stereochemistry
Optical isomerism: chirality, enantiomers, racemic mixture
Grignard reagent: preparation and synthetic applications
Aldehydes and Ketones: nucleophilic addition reactions mechanism
Aldol condensation and Cannizzaro reaction
Carboxylic Acids: acidity and factors affecting it, Hell-Volhard-Zelinsky reaction
Amines: Hoffmann bromamide degradation reaction
Diazonium salts: preparation and synthetic applications in organic chemistry
Solid State: types of unit cells, packing efficiency calculations
Crystal defects: Frenkel and Schottky defects, n-type and p-type semiconductors
Solutions: van't Hoff factor and abnormal molar masses
Henry's law and its applications to gas solubility
Thermodynamics: Gibbs free energy and its relation to equilibrium constant
Third law of thermodynamics and absolute entropy
